summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the @envelop/rate-limiter plugin's onExecute enforcement is never invoked for GraphQL subscription operations. Queries to a @rateLimit(max:3) field are correctly throttled after 3 calls, while subscriptions to the same rate-limited field succeed without limit.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that 5 queries to a @rateLimit(max:3) field result in rate-limit errors for the 4th and 5th attempts, while 5 subscriptions to the same field all succeed, proving the rate limiter is bypassed for subscriptions.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the target repository, created a test GraphQL schema with @rateLimit directives on both query and subscription fields, and confirmed that queries are rate-limited at the configured threshold while subscriptions bypass rate limiting entirely.
verification_method: The exploit sends exactly 5 queries and 5 subscriptions to a @rateLimit(max:3) field. The verification checks that (a) at least one query is rate-limited with HTTP 429 status, and (b) zero subscriptions are rate-limited. The script exits with code 0 only when this contrast is observed, confirming the bypass.
evidence_produced_by_run: Query operations 1-3 succeed with data; queries 4-5 return rate-limit errors with HTTP 429 extensions.
All 5 subscription operations succeed without any rate-limit error, each returning an AsyncIterator.
The exit code 0 confirms the vulnerability signal was observed.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
